original_theorem:
If a nonempty set $S$ of real numbers  is bounded above$,$ then
$\sup S$ is the unique real number $\beta$ such that
\begin{alist}
\item % (a)
 $x\le\beta$ for all $x$ in $S;$
\item % (b)
 if $\epsilon>0$ $($no matter how small$)$$,$ there is an $x_0$ in
$S$ such that
$x_0>
\beta-\epsilon.$
\end{alist}
theorem_translation:
```lean4
theorem real_sup_unique_characterized (S : Set έΕζ) (h_nonempty : S.Nonempty) (h_bdd_above : BddAbove S) :
    έΙΔ! ╬▓ : έΕζ, (έΙΑ x έΙΙ S, x έΚν ╬▓) έΙπ (έΙΑ ╬╡ > 0, έΙΔ xέΓΑ έΙΙ S, xέΓΑ > ╬▓ - ╬╡) := by
```
